title: fix(enter): restore burst accumulation in PollenRateLimiter
author: fisventurous

gist.headline: Patience is now a currency
gist.blurb: The rate limiter was having a bit of amnesia about your idle time. We've reminded it that waiting should be rewarded with a nice little burst of speed when you return.
gist.summary: Fixed a regression in the PollenRateLimiter where accumulated idle time was being discarded on new requests instead of credited as burst capacity.
gist.impact: Users who idle between requests can now utilize a burst of activity upon returning, rather than being throttled immediately.
gist.visual_concept: A visual metaphor for 'stored energy' or 'accumulated time'. A glass tank or battery filling up with lime green liquid (pollen/energy) while the bee rests. This represents the 'idle time' now counting towards a full tank of request credits.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot napping peacefully on a large leaf next to a glass tank filling with glowing lime green (#ecf874) honey. A retro gauge on the tank indicates it is charging up. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
